name: St. Petersburg Neighborhood Housing Services, Inc.Dba Neighborhood Home Solutions
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit housing counselor providing first-time homebuyer education, pre-purchase counseling, and foreclosure prevention assistance to St. Petersburg residents.
description_long:
Neighborhood Home Solutions (operating as St. Petersburg Neighborhood Housing Services, Inc.) is a nonprofit organization based in St. Petersburg, Florida, dedicated to expanding homeownership access and providing financial stability to underserved communities. The organization operates under the HUD-approved housing counselor framework, meaning their counselors meet federal certification standards for housing and credit guidance.

The organization offers three primary service lines: a First Time Homebuyer Program that provides the mandatory Certificate of Education required by mortgage lenders, along with mortgage readiness assessment and financing guidance; Pre-Purchase Counseling conducted one-on-one with HUD-certified counselors who develop personalized action plans and provide tools for home financing, ownership, and maintenance; and Foreclosure Prevention services where counselors communicate directly with lenders on behalf of homeowners to explore alternatives and prevent loss of home. All services are designed to meet clients where they are financially and help build pathways to homeownership.

What distinguishes Neighborhood Home Solutions is their demonstrated track record of client success—the website features multiple documented case studies of first-time homebuyers who achieved homeownership after completing their programs (Grayson and Bryan, Catherine, Danny Gibble Jr and daughter Lily). The organization also engages in community development beyond housing, including environmental initiatives like the NHS Rain Garden Project in partnership with Florida Suncoast Sierra Club and local community groups, demonstrating a holistic approach to neighborhood stabilization.

As a free nonprofit service, there are no financial barriers to accessing their core counseling and education programs. The primary limitation is geographic scope—services appear focused on St. Petersburg and the surrounding Pinellas County area. Additionally, while they provide education and counseling, they do not directly fund mortgages or loans; they prepare clients to qualify for financing through traditional lenders. The organization's capacity may be limited as a smaller regional nonprofit.
pros: HUD-certified counselors meet federal housing counseling standards for credibility
First Time Homebuyer Program provides mandatory Certificate of Education required by mortgage lenders
Pre-Purchase Counseling is personalized and one-on-one, with tailored action plans rather than generic courses
Foreclosure Prevention includes direct lender communication on behalf of homeowners
All core services are provided free of charge with no financial barriers
Documented client success stories showing real homeownership outcomes
Community-engaged organization with environmental and neighborhood stability initiatives
cons: Geographic service area appears limited to St. Petersburg/Pinellas County region
Does not provide direct mortgage funding or loans—only education and preparation
Capacity may be limited as a smaller regional nonprofit compared to national organizations
Website lacks detailed information about wait times, counselor availability, or service timelines
best_for: First-time homebuyers in St. Petersburg/Pinellas County seeking required mortgage education certificates
Homeowners facing foreclosure who need to negotiate with lenders and understand prevention options
Renters on fixed incomes seeking to improve financial readiness for homeownership
diagnosis: Neighborhood Home Solutions is best for first-time homebuyers and at-risk homeowners in the St. Petersburg area seeking free, HUD-certified counseling to prepare for or maintain homeownership. The main caveat is that they provide education and counseling only—they cannot directly finance a home purchase, so clients must still qualify for traditional mortgage lending after completing their programs.
services: First Time Homebuyer Program with Certificate of Education
Mortgage readiness assessment and financial counseling
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1600 Dr M L King St N, St Petersburg, FL 33701
